Title: Your veeam license has been expired on ISI-HQ-VBR-01
Notes: During the Veeam backup check, I noticed that the VSPC portal was showing a warning that the Veeam license had expired. To resolve this, I connected to the ISI-HQ-VBR-01 server and verified that the license was running in the grace period. I then logged into the Veeam Pulse portal, updated the license expiry date to the end of March, downloaded the required license file, and initiated the installation process. Once the installation was completed, I restarted the VSPC agent service on the server and verified that the updated license is now visible on the VSPC portal.

Title: SERVER Alert - Critical - ISI-HQ-VBR-01.isi.local: (192.168.1.20) - Disk Utilization is 99%, threshold value for this monitor is 90%
Notes: As per the alert connected to ISI-HQ-VBR-01, download and install TreeSize, a disk space management tool that provides a detailed overview of the disk usage across folders and files on your system. After installation, TreeSize was run to analyse the disk. TreeSize scans the system and generates a report showing the size of all folders and files in a hierarchical structure, so carefully review all folders and files occupying excessive space. Based on the review, we proceeded to clear unnecessary files that were identified as no longer needed. This included Clearing Temporary Files: Files in the system's temporary folder are often created by applications during installation, updates, or other processes. Clearing these temporary files frees up space. Recycle Bin: emptied the Recycle Bin, where deleted files are temporarily stored. This ensured that deleted files were permanently removed from the disk and no longer took up space. Downloaded Items: System error memory dump files, Old Windows updates, previous Windows installations, and other system cache files. 35% of the space is free. Now Sufficient Free Space Available.

Title: Finding the mac address for the Vmware Esxi.
Notes: There was an issue where the VMware Esxi was able access from only one computer and was not able to connect from other computers. Due Sunday's power outage server was on dynamic and it was able to detect from the scanning the IP. Gurdeep and I did check all the configurations. We found out some computers had issues with TLS version, we troubleshooted that. I reserved the Ip address of 5 virtual machines, switch from the router. Later configured the switch made some changes including the IP. Reserved the Ip on router. Assigned an new IP to VMware esxi and found the mac address. Reserved the IP address. Changed few devices from dynamic to static Ip address.  Issue was resolved. Gurdeep verified that we can do the backup for ISI, will be done after work hours. Sharon from ISI was notified with the steps taken to resolve the issue.

Title: ISI backups were failed
Notes: Investigated intermittent and inconsistent access to ESXi host management interface. Identified root cause as an IP address conflict between the ESXi management interface (vmk0) and the Zyxel GS1900 switch, both configured with the same IP address. Remotely accessed the Zyxel switch, reassigned its management IP to a verified free address, and confirmed successful connectivity on the new IP. Cleared ARP conflicts and validated stable access to the ESXi host from multiple machines. Reviewed the network to confirm no additional IP conflicts, verified available IP addresses, and implemented DHCP/IP reservations for all virtual machines and critical network devices to prevent recurrence. Issue fully resolved and environment stabilized.

Title: ISI front desk is having the issue with Credit Card terminal
Notes: As per Rishad they are having the issue with Credit Card terminal Reviewed and troubleshot credit card terminal connectivity issues where the device functioned correctly when connected directly to the modem but failed when placed behind the Netgear R6400 router. Verified router configuration including SIP ALG, SPI firewall, DMZ, NAT behavior, and existing port forwarding rules. Confirmed the terminal was correctly configured using DHCP with an IP reservation. Identified inconsistent DNS configuration across the environment, with different systems receiving multiple DNS servers (including legacy and public DNS), which could impact secure outbound payment processing. Standardized the environment by confirming Windows DHCP service on ISI-HQ-AD-01 (192.168.1.27) as the sole DHCP authority and updating DHCP Scope Option 006 to provide only the Active Directory–integrated DNS server. Verified all servers, virtual machines, and workstations are now receiving the correct DHCP configuration, default gateway, and DNS settings. Validated DNS resolution from multiple endpoints. Environment is now in a stable and compliant state; pending end-user confirmation of successful payment transactions before removing temporary router workarounds (DMZ/port forwarding) and closing the issue.

Title: credit card issue
